zh.lifeSummary: 當Ellen Daniell於1340年出生在英國爾達雷斯波利時，她的父親William Daniell24歲，母親Clemence Norreys18歲。她於1356年與John De Marbury在英國英格蘭賀福爾維奧布利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國賀福爾利沙拉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ellen Daniell于1340年出生在英国爾達雷斯波利时，她的父亲William Daniell24岁，母亲Clemence Norreys18岁。她于1356年与John De Marbury在英国英格兰賀福爾維奧布利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国賀福爾利沙拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Daniell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (Galway): variant of   Daniel  .

variants.name: Daniel
meanings: English, French, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Romanian, Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Breton (rarely Le Daniel); Hungarian (Dániel): from the Biblical personal name Daniel, Syriac (Assyrian/Chaldean) Dānīʾēl, Hungarian Dániel (from Hebrew Daniyyel ‘God is my judge’), borne by a major prophet in the Bible. The chief factor influencing the popularity of the personal name (and hence the frequency of the surname) was undoubtedly the dramatic story in the Book of Daniel recounting the prophet's steadfast adherence to his religious faith despite pressure and persecution from the Mesopotamian kings in whose court he served: Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, and Darius. The name was also borne by a 2nd-century Christian martyr and by a 9th-century hermit, the legend of whose lives were popular among Christians during the Middle Ages. Among Orthodox Christians in Eastern Europe the name was also popular as being that of a 4th-century Persian martyr, who was venerated in the Orthodox Church. In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.

Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of McDaniel , which is actually a variant of McDonnell , from the Gaelic form of Irish Donal (equivalent to Scottish Donald), erroneously associated with the Biblical personal name Daniel. See also O'Donnell .
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Danijel: from the Biblical personal name Danijel ‘Daniel’ (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Ellen Daniell was born in 1340, in Daresbury, Cheshire, England, her father, William Daniell, was 24 and her mother, Clemence Norreys, was 18. She married John De Marbury in 1356, in Weobley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Lyonshall, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ellen Daniell est née en 1340, à Daresbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William Daniell, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Clemence Norreys, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John De Marbury en 1356, à Weobley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Lyonshall, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ellen Daniell 1340 in Daresbury, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Daniell 24 und ihre Mutter Clemence Norreys 18. Sie hat 1356 in Weobley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom John De Marbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Lyonshall, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ellen Daniell è nata il nel 1340 nel Daresbury, Cheshire, England, sua padre, William Daniell, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Clemence Norreys, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato John De Marbury nel 1356, nel Weobley, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Lyonshall, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Ellen Daniellが1340年にイギリスチェシュアイアダレスブリーで生まれた時，父William Daniellは24歳，母Clemence Norreysは18歳であった。1356年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウエオブリーでJohn De Marburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアリオンシャルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen Daniell(이)가 1340년 영국 체실르 더레스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Daniell(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Clemence Norreys(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 베오블리에서 John De Marbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 허퍼드실르 리샤에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ellen Daniell родилась в 1340 г. в местности даресбури, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Daniell было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Clemence Norreys, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John De Marbury в 1356 г. в местности веобли, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности леншолл, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ellen Daniell nació en 1340, en Daresbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Daniell, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Clemence Norreys, tenía 18 años.
